issues: The draft says Sockeyes 'trailed 2-1 after two periods' but the OCR says they trailed 1-0 after the first period and 2-1 after the second — the draft's phrasing is correct on the deficit but omits the first-period trail detail; minor but not an error per se.
The draft states the series is the 'Tier 2 western final series', which matches the OCR.
The headline is AI-generated and not from the OCR — no clear headline is present in the OCR text for this article.
The draft omits the detail that the fourth game will be played in Richmond on Saturday.

The draft incorrectly says Richmond 'rallied from a 2-1 deficit after two periods' — the OCR says they trailed 1-0 after one period and 2-1 after two periods, meaning they were behind (not level) after each intermission; the framing implies a late comeback but the OCR does not support 'rallied' specifically.
The OCR clarifies Humboldt Broncos are the Saskatchewan champions, which is not mentioned in the draft.
The draft notes the overtime period without specifying it was a 10-minute overtime period, as stated in the OCR.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Tony Bobbitt scored 1:04 into a 10-minute overtime to give the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 win over the Humboldt Broncos, taking a 2-1 lead in the Centennial Cup Tier 2 western final series.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Tony Bobbitt scored at 1:04 of a 10-minute overtime period to give the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 win over the Saskatchewan-champion Humboldt Broncos in a Centennial Cup junior hockey playoff game. The victory gave Richmond a 2-1 lead in the best-of-seven Tier 2 western final series, with Game 4 scheduled in Richmond on Saturday. Dave Tomlinson, Mike Claringbull, and Rob Clarke also scored for the Sockeyes, who were outshot 39-23 and trailed 1-0 after the first period and 2-1 after the second. Bill McDougall (two goals) and Curtis Chamberlin scored for Humboldt.
review.notes: No clear article headline is present in the OCR — the AI-generated headline should be removed or treated as a descriptive label only. The article is a CP wire brief. Key details added: 10-minute OT specification, first-period deficit, Game 4 location, Broncos identified as Saskatchewan champions, and Humboldt scorers.
